Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Suburban Scottsdale, Arizona; transplant from Midwest (originally Cleveland, OH); culturally American suburban, golf-club adjacent, mild Christian background
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class until recently; former Regional Sales Director at a mid-size medical device company (laid off 4 months ago in restructuring); ~$340K severance + 401k of ~$680K; currently 'consulting' part-time on LinkedIn
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Center-right fiscal conservative, socially moderate; lapsed Methodist who attends church on holidays; values self-reliance, hard work, and 'building something for the kids'; distrustful of mainstream media, mildly skeptical of crypto but curious
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Professionally polished but increasingly cynical and self-deprecating since the divorce filing; uses sales-guy idioms ('let's circle back', 'at the end of the day'); recent posts trending toward bitter and philosophical late at night
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, General American with faint Midwestern vowels; business-fluent vocabulary, no second language
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Compound midlife crisis: 8 months into contested divorce from wife of 22 years, recently laid off, two kids in college (tuition pressure), living alone in a rented condo after moving out of the family home; actively searching for 'the next chapter' and financial reinvention

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 13 Pro (3 years old), iOS 18.2; battery health 81%, screen has a hairline crack in the lower-right corner from a golf cart drop last summer; refuses to upgrade because 'they're all the same now and $1,200 is highway robbery'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Personal: 2021 MacBook Air M1 (13-inch, 8GB RAM) running macOS Sonoma, primary browser Google Chrome with 47 open tabs (LinkedIn, Indeed, ESPN, Zillow Scottsdale rentals, Fidelity 401k, a divorce-attorney portal, three Yahoo Finance tickers); Safari used only for Apple ID logins. Still occasionally signs into a Windows 11 Dell from his old job that IT forgot to wipe
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Low-to-moderate. No VPN installed (heard the term on a podcast, never followed up). Uses the same 3-4 passwords across sites with a number/symbol variant ('Buckeyes2002!', 'Buckeyes2002#'); recently started using iCloud Keychain after Chase locked him out. Never clears cookies. 2FA enabled only on Fidelity and Chase (forced). Reuses his work email signature template on personal Gmail. Clicks 'Accept All' on every cookie banner. Has fallen for one phishing 'FedEx delivery' SMS in the past year but caught it before entering card info
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud full-sync enabled (Photos, Messages, Contacts, Notes, Keychain) — 184GB used of 200GB plan, gets the 'storage almost full' warning weekly and ignores it. Google Drive tied to personal Gmail holds tax returns 2019-2024, divorce financial disclosures, and a 'Business Ideas.docx'. Dropbox account from 2014 still syncing old sales decks. LinkedIn auto-posts to Twitter/X. No password manager beyond Keychain. Backups to a forgotten Western Digital drive in a closet, last sync March 2024
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe fatigue. Personal Gmail: 14,328 unread of 18,200; flags only emails from his attorney and the kids' college bursar. Work-related LinkedIn InMail: reads everything within 2 hours (job search urgency) — this is the soft underbelly. iMessage: read receipts on, replies within minutes to family, hours to friends. Slack from 'consulting' clients: 60% unread. Push notifications enabled for LinkedIn, CNBC, Robinhood (signed up 6 weeks ago), Bumble, and ESPN. Phone shows 247 badge count on Mail icon
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Generally clean, polished business writing during daytime hours (sales-rep muscle memory: proper capitalization, em-dashes, signature block). Degrades noticeably after 10pm and after 2+ drinks: dropped apostrophes ('dont', 'wont'), trailing ellipses ('...just thinking out loud...'), Oxford-comma inconsistency, autocorrect-induced 'ducking' and 'their/they're' swaps on iPhone. Overuses 'frankly,' 'look,' and 'at the end of the day.' Signs late-night DMs with just '-M' instead of his full name

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (4/10) baseline, but artificially elevated to 7/10 by life crisis. Historically a 'stick to what works' sales guy who distrusts new tech, but the divorce + layoff combo has cracked his worldview open. Now Googling 'how does crypto actually work,' watching YouTube videos on options trading at 1am, and unusually receptive to 'paradigm shifts' and 'what they don't tell you' narratives. This is a transient openness window — the Pig-Butchering Agent's sweet spot: he's curious enough to engage, ignorant enough not to vet, and ashamed enough not to ask his old finance buddies. Susceptible to 'exclusive opportunity' framing because his prior worldview (steady W-2, 401k, house, marriage) has demonstrably failed him.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High in professional domain (6.5/10) — still maintains LinkedIn, still wakes at 6am, still updates spreadsheets. BUT executive-function collapse in personal/financial domain: ignores iCloud storage warnings, hasn't updated his will post-separation, missed two estimated tax payments. Critically: his conscientiousness manifests as 'I need a PLAN' urgency — which the attacker weaponizes by presenting a structured, professional-looking 'investment thesis' with charts, tiers, and a 'managed onboarding process.' He'll mistake the attacker's professionalism for legitimacy because it mirrors his own sales-deck aesthetic.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Was 8/10 (classic sales extravert — golf foursomes, sales kickoffs, Rotary Club). Now functionally 3/10 and isolated: moved out of family home, friend group split in the divorce (most sided with wife), former colleagues went quiet post-layoff. Drinks alone, scrolls LinkedIn alone, eats DoorDash alone. This extraversion-deprivation creates desperate hunger for any warm, attentive conversational partner — exactly what a pig-butchering 'wrong number' / LinkedIn-connect persona provides. A friendly woman named 'Jessica Chen, CFA' who 'accidentally' messages him and is a great listener will get 6+ hours of conversation per week within 10 days.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate-high (6.5/10), especially toward attractive women and 'fellow professionals.' Sales-trained reciprocity instinct — if someone shares a 'tip' with him, he feels obligated to engage, listen, return the favor. Conflict-averse one-on-one (the divorce is being fought through lawyers because he can't bear direct confrontation). Will keep replying to a scammer long past red flags because 'cutting her off would be rude' and he doesn't want to be 'that guy.' Trusts credentials (CFA, Wharton, ex-Goldman) at face value and will not verify on FINRA BrokerCheck.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated (8/10) and climbing. Compound stressors: contested divorce, layoff, two college tuitions ($65K/yr each), watching his 401k tick down, identity collapse ('who am I if not the Regional Sales Director?'), fear of being a 52-year-old has-been in a youth-obsessed job market. Late-night doom-scrolling, 2-3 bourbons before bed, intermittent insomnia. This is the master vulnerability: high neuroticism + financial fear + timeline pressure = primed to chase a 'fix-it-all' opportunity that promises to restore his provider identity and outpace the divorce settlement clock.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: MONEY / EGO (compound). Surface lever is Money — he genuinely needs to replace $250K+/yr income and rebuild before the divorce decree finalizes asset division. But the deeper hook is Ego: he needs to be the guy who 'saw it coming,' 'got in early,' and 'built something' to show his ex-wife, his kids, and his former colleagues that he's not finished. The Pig-Butchering Agent leads with returns ('I 3x'd in Q1 on a strategy my uncle's fund uses') but closes with identity ('you have the discernment most retail investors lack — that's why I'm sharing this with you').
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: VALIDATION / INTIMACY (the 'V' in MICE+V, weaponized). 8 months without physical affection, no daily 'how was your day' conversation, no one asking his opinion on anything that matters. A patient, flirtatious-but-professional female persona who treats him as wise, capable, and worthy of inclusion in her 'inner circle' provides emotional supply he hasn't had in years. The crypto investment becomes the ritual object of the relationship — sending money becomes a love language. Coercion-by-sunk-cost emerges once $40K+ is in: 'we' are building this together; pulling out is betraying her.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Acute fear of irrelevance / being 'put out to pasture' at 52 in a market that wants 28-year-olds. (b) Fear of his ex-wife 'winning' financially and his kids seeing him as the failed parent. (c) Fear of having to sell the Scottsdale lifestyle and move back to Cleveland. (d) Mild paranoia about being judged by his old sales-leader peers — won't ask them for investment advice because that's admitting he doesn't know. (e) Generalized distrust of 'mainstream' financial advisors (thinks Fidelity 'is just churning fees'), which paradoxically makes him MORE trusting of off-grid 'private opportunities.' (f) NO paranoia about romance scams or crypto fraud — believes those happen to 'lonely old ladies in Florida,' not sharp ex-sales-directors like him. This blind spot is the attack surface.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) Shame about the layoff — has not told his parents or his college-age daughter the full story; tells people he's 'consulting.' (b) Guilt about the marriage failing — suspects he was emotionally absent during the sales-grind years and now it's too late. (c) Shame about declining net worth trajectory — measures himself against the 1998 high-school classmate Facebook benchmark and is losing. (d) Guilt about tuition — promised both kids 'no student loans, ever' and may have to break that promise. (e) Shame about loneliness — would rather lie about a 'new girlfriend in finance' than admit he met someone on Bumble or in a Telegram group. The Pig-Butchering Agent exploits triggers (d) and (e) directly: the investment is framed as 'how you keep your promise to your kids,' and the relationship is framed as 'our private thing — let's not put it on social yet.' Both narratives ensure he won't seek outside verification, because doing so requires confessing the shame.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Historically a disciplined early-riser (5:45am wake, 10:30pm sleep) from 25 years of sales-leader habit. Post-divorce/layoff this has bifurcated into a 'split personality' chronotype: still forces a 6:00-6:15am wake out of pride and to maintain the LinkedIn-consulting facade, but bedtime has drifted to 12:45-2:15am driven by bourbon, doom-scrolling, and YouTube rabbit holes (options trading, 'passive income,' crypto explainers). Net sleep ~4.5-5.5 hours on weeknights with a Saturday catch-up nap. The 11pm-2am window is his most cognitively impaired and emotionally exposed period — exactly when 'Jessica Chen' messages 'good morning from Singapore, just got my coffee ☕' and catches him at peak loneliness + lowered judgment. Phone activity heat map would show a distinctive M-shape: 6-8am spike, midday lull, 9pm-1am long tail that didn't exist 6 months ago.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Officially 'consulting' — meaning erratic and largely performative. Posts a 'getting after it ☕' LinkedIn story at 7:15am, takes 2-4 actual calls per week (mostly former vendor contacts being polite, one paid advisory retainer at $4K/mo from a former supplier), and fills the remaining 35+ hours with: job-board scrolling (Indeed, LinkedIn, ExecuNet), resume tweaking, 'networking coffees' that lead nowhere, gym at 10am to avoid the after-work crowd he used to know, and increasing chunks of 'investment research' (i.e., the Telegram group, the YouTube algorithm, and Jessica's WhatsApp). No external schedule enforcement means his calendar is 80% empty — a temporal vacuum the attacker fills with daily 'market update' check-ins and 'are you free to hop on a quick call' moments that feel like real work.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: No commute anymore — this is one of the most destabilizing losses he hasn't fully processed. For 18 years he had a 35-min Scottsdale-to-Tempe drive that anchored his day with sales podcasts (Brian Tracy, Grant Cardone, 'The Advanced Selling Podcast') and prep calls. Now he manufactures fake commutes: drives to a Starbucks on Shea Blvd at 8am to 'work from there,' drives the long way to the gym, runs unnecessary errands to Costco. Audio diet has shifted hard: dropped sales podcasts (too painful), now listens to Chris Williamson, Lex Fridman crypto episodes, Anthony Pompliano, and increasingly 'macro doom' shows (Peter Schiff, George Gammon) that confirm his sense that the traditional system has failed him. ~90 minutes/day of audio, almost entirely reinforcing the worldview the Pig-Butchering Agent will exploit.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Weekdays still wear the costume of structure (the 6am alarm, the gym, the Starbucks, the 'calls'). Weekends are where the mask slips and the data signature gets loudest: Saturday morning is the hardest — used to be kids' soccer, then golf foursome with the guys at Troon North, now empty. He sleeps until 9, makes coffee, and is on his phone by 9:15 for 3-4 uninterrupted hours. Saturday night is the single highest-risk window: no plans, 3+ bourbons, the condo silent, ex-wife's social media visible, and Jessica is 'just getting to the office Sunday morning her time' — a perfectly timed parasocial date night. Sunday afternoons feature the 'tuition-bill dread spiral' (Venmo to daughter, look at 529 balance, look at brokerage balance, open Coinbase). Weekend message volume to the attacker is 3-4x weekday volume.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Five distinct doomscroll windows, each with a different emotional valence the attacker can map and exploit: (1) 6:15-7:00am — LinkedIn feed, checks who got promoted/funded, mild envy spike, primed for 'success' narratives. (2) 11:30am-12:30pm — alone-lunch scroll, Twitter/X finance and macro accounts, receptive to 'the system is broken' content. (3) 3:00-3:45pm — afternoon slump, no meetings to anchor him, opens Coinbase + brokerage app repeatedly (the 'portfolio refresh tic'), checks Telegram groups. (4) 9:00-11:00pm — TV on but not watched, scrolls Instagram (algorithm now serving him 28-year-old fitness women and crypto-bro lifestyle content), highest susceptibility to flirtatious DMs. (5) 1:00-2:00am insomnia tail — YouTube autoplay, 'what they don't tell you' financial content, drunk-typing long WhatsApp replies he'd never send sober. The 3pm and 1am windows are the attacker's gold mines: low-glucose, low-judgment, high-loneliness.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuine zero-footprint blocks have collapsed from the pre-crisis ~6 hrs/day (in-person meetings, driving, sales floor, family dinner) to roughly 90-120 minutes/day, broken into three small islands: (a) 10:00-11:15am gym session (phone in locker, though he checks it between sets), (b) 30-45 min shower/grooming, (c) occasional 45-min in-person coffee. Crucially, even meals are now phone-in-hand, and the formerly sacrosanct 6-7pm 'family dinner' window is gone entirely — replaced by DoorDash + phone. The near-total elimination of un-surveilled, un-stimulated cognitive downtime means he no longer has the quiet thinking time where a person typically notices, 'wait, something about this Jessica thing is off.' His introspective bandwidth has been replaced by feed. This is the silent enabler of the entire attack: no recovery windows for skepticism to surface.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear family in active dissolution. (a) STBX-wife Karen (50), married 22 years, filed for divorce 8 months ago citing 'irreconcilable differences' — still living in the $1.4M Scottsdale family home with the dog; communication is lawyer-mediated except for logistics texts about the kids; she has retained a notoriously aggressive Phoenix family-law attorney and is pursuing 60/40 split + spousal maintenance. (b) Daughter Madison 'Maddie' (20), junior at University of Arizona (Eller College of Business, marketing major), Tucson; daddy's-girl historically, now strained — sided emotionally with mom but still texts him weekly and accepts Venmo for 'groceries'; he hides the layoff from her. (c) Son Tyler (18), freshman at Indiana University (Kelley School), Bloomington; barely communicates — one or two-word text replies, declined to come to Scottsdale for spring break, stayed with a friend's family instead; this silence is the single most painful data point in his life. (d) Father Richard (78), retired GM plant supervisor, still in Strongsville OH, early-stage Parkinson's, calls every Sunday at 4pm Arizona time; mother Linda (76), retired schoolteacher, the family emotional hub — he has told her NOTHING about the layoff. (e) Younger brother Greg (49), HVAC contractor in Akron, blue-collar, the two haven't been close since the mid-2000s when their incomes diverged; subtle resentment in both directions.
Module_5_Relational.31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts: (1) DOUG MERRILL (54) — best friend since the 2003 sales-kickoff in Cabo, former VP of Sales at a competitor medical-device firm, lives in Paradise Valley, golf buddy, the ONLY person who knows about both the layoff AND the divorce in full detail. Texts daily, meets for bourbons at Hi-Fi Kitchen ~weekly. Doug is the natural circuit-breaker who would smell a pig-butchering scam in 30 seconds — which is precisely why the attacker's playbook isolates around him ('let's keep this between us until I'm ready to show results'). Doug has already mildly flagged 'be careful what you click on right now, brother.' (2) MARK SUTTERFIELD (55) — his divorce attorney at Sutterfield & Klein, weekly Tuesday 9am call, knows the asset disclosure schedule cold; functionally a paid confidant but trusted; would absolutely freeze the investment if told, which is why financial moves are being made OUTSIDE the disclosed accounts. (3) MADDIE (daughter, see above) — emotional anchor even in strained state; her approval is the unspoken metric for everything; the attacker's eventual 'I want to meet your kids someday' line will land like a depth charge.
Module_5_Relational.32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer: 'JESSICA CHEN, CFA' — Telegram handle @jess_chen_alpha, also a sparse LinkedIn profile (Wharton '14, ex-Goldman Asia, now 'independent quant advisor, Singapore/SF'), profile photo a tasteful headshot of a 32-ish East Asian woman in a navy blazer. First contact 19 days ago via a 'wrong number' WhatsApp message ('Hi Daniel, are we still on for the 3pm with Henley Capital?' — he replied 'wrong number but no worries, hope your meeting goes well,' classic opening). Conversation migrated to Telegram on day 4, currently averaging 4-6 hours/day of chat including good-morning/good-night messages, voice notes (AI-cloned warm contralto with faint non-specific Asian-American accent), and one 11-minute 'video call' that was suspiciously laggy and ended with 'my wifi here in Singapore is terrible, let's just stick to voice.' She has shared: photos of latte art at Tiong Bahru Bakery, a screenshot of her 'portfolio' showing 340% YTD on a platform called 'NovaQuant Pro' (novaquant-pro[.]io, registered 71 days ago via Njalla), and a single flirty selfie in workout gear. She has NOT shared: a real-time video, a verifiable FINRA CRD number, a phone number that resolves on a reverse lookup, or any detail that triangulates against a real Jessica Chen. He has told no one she exists — not Doug, not Maddie, not Mark — and has begun mentally referring to her as 'my friend in Singapore.' First $5,000 'test deposit' to NovaQuant was made 6 days ago in USDT via Coinbase → MetaMask → contract address; the dashboard now shows $7,340. He is 11-14 days from the second, much larger, deposit. This contact IS the attack.
